reforms.description: The government plans to increase fiscal support for business research and development to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to increase fiscal support for business R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for driving innovation and improving productivity across the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Increase fiscal support for business research and development.

reforms.description: The government aims to maintain or increase long-term support for successful collaboration between research institutions and industry to foster innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening collaboration between research institutions and industry is expected to enhance knowledge transfer and commercialization of research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to maintain or increase support for collaboration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative builds on existing frameworks to enhance collaboration, which is vital for innovation but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Maintain or increase long-term support for successful collaboration between research institutions and industry.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government should increase its support for innovation by increasing R&D grants or re-introducing an R&D tax credit, as current grants are capped at NZD25 million per year, which may discourage firm growth.
reforms.component_name: R&D Grants and Tax Credit Reintroduction
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding can enhance firm productivity and stimulate economic growth through innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should increase its support for innovation by increasing R&D grants or re-introducing an R&D tax credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the government's approach to supporting R&D, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The NZ government should increase its support for innovation by increasing R&D grants or re-introducing an R&D tax credit.

reforms.description: The Innovative New Zealand package provided additional science and innovation funding in the 2016 and 2017 budgets, including increased funding for R&D grants to meet rising demand while maintaining the same rate of support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing support for R&D is expected to boost productivity by addressing market failures and enhancing innovation capacity among firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The package was included in the budgets for 2016 and 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public funding for R&D, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Innovative New Zealand package provided additional science and innovation funding in the 2016 and 2017 budgets, including increased funding for R&D grants.

reforms.description: A new Action Plan under New Zealand’s strategy for boosting Māori economic performance aims to grow a more productive, innovative, and internationally connected Māori economic sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative is expected to enhance productivity and innovation within the Māori economy, contributing positively to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A new Action Plan will be released this year.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Action Plan represents a significant effort to enhance the economic performance of a key demographic, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A new Action Plan under New Zealand’s strategy for boosting Māori economic performance will be released this year.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has committed to increasing its expenditure on R&D to enhance business research and development support, addressing the low levels of government funding compared to other OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to stimulate private sector R&D investment and enhance overall innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to increasing its expenditure on R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in government policy towards enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for improving innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The NZ government has committed to increasing its expenditure on R&D.

reforms.description: The introduction of an R&D tax loss credit allows firms to cash out net losses for R&D expenditures, providing a financial incentive for businesses to invest in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This tax incentive is designed to lower the financial barriers for firms engaging in R&D, thereby promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax loss credit took effect in the 2015-16 tax year.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is an adjustment to existing tax policy rather than a complete overhaul, thus it is categorized as a significant but narrower reform.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D tax loss credit that took effect in the 2015-16 tax year does provide favourable treatment for R&D expenditure.

reforms.description: In April 2017, the New Zealand government announced changes to the Skilled Migrant Category, including the introduction of remuneration thresholds aimed at attracting higher-skilled immigrants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting higher-skilled immigrants, the reform is expected to enhance the overall skill level of the workforce, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a series of changes, including adding remuneration thresholds.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment to immigration policy aimed at improving the skill composition of the workforce, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2017 the government announced a series of changes, including adding remuneration thresholds for the Skilled Migrant Category.

reforms.description: The High-Performance Working Initiative (HPWI) aims to provide business coaching for small to medium-sized businesses to enhance work practices, thereby improving performance and increasing employee engagement and satisfaction.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving workplace practices, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and employee satisfaction, contributing positively to economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is actively promoting better skills utilization through workplace innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance workplace practices, which can lead to improved productivity and employee engagement across various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The High-Performance Working Initiative (HPWI) provides business coaching for small to medium-sized businesses to help improve work practices.
